introduction: Database security is a critical aspect of modern information systems that ensures the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data. This virtual lab provides interactive experiments and simulations to help students understand database security concepts such as authentication, access control, encryption, auditing, and attack prevention in relational database systems.

experiments.name: Implementation and Analysis of User Authentication Mechanisms in Database Systems
Design and Implementation of Virtual Private Databases for Row-Level Access Control
Auditing and Monitoring User Activities in Database Systems
Securing Sensitive Information through Database Encryption Techniques
SQL Injection Attack Simulation and Mitigation Techniques
Configuring Secure Connectivity for Encrypted Client–Server Database Communication

UG: Students in Computer Science, Information Technology, and Cyber Security

objective: To enable students to understand database security principles, experiment with access control models, implement encryption techniques, detect vulnerabilities, and analyze security threats such as SQL injection through interactive simulations.

courseAlignment.description: The syllabi of this lab aligns to the following universities in India.
universities: Anna University – Database Security & Access Control
SRM Institute of Science and Technology – DBMS with Security Components
VIT, Vellore – Database Systems & Cyber Security
AICTE – PCC CS-505: Introduction to Database Systems
AICTE – PEC CS-601: Introductory Cyber Security
